whyToday.title: Why your plan today
whyToday.deloadDesc: Your body needs recovery. Calories at maintenance, training volume −50%.
whyToday.returnDesc: Easing back in: lighter sessions, soft deficit (TDEE × 0.92).
whyToday.illnessDesc: Recovery −0.15 and calories −5% (not −20%) until your body stabilizes.
whyToday.travelDesc: No penalty. Resume when you're back — your queue is paused.
whyToday.injuryDesc: Training paused. We'll modify volume when you return.
whyToday.lutealDesc: Algorithm added +150 kcal carbs and reduced training intensity 5% — synced with your cycle.
whyToday.menstrualDesc: Weight isn't a reliable signal today — we shift adaptations to next week.
whyToday.fatigueDesc: Low sleep or high stress detected. Calories at maintenance — no extra deficit today.
whyToday.metabolicDesc: Liquid calories > 10% of budget. Plan progression paused 3 days for accurate signal.
whyToday.hydrationDesc: Hydration target wasn't met. Macro changes blocked for 24h until you catch up.
whyToday.footer: Adaptations are automatic — focus on the plan, the algo handles the rest.

cycle.menstruation.training: Focus on light exercises, yoga, and walks. Reduce intensity and listen to your body.
cycle.follicular.training: Energy is rising! Great time for HIIT, heavier weights, and new PRs. Give it your all!
cycle.ovulation.training: Peak strength and endurance! Group workouts, HIIT, and challenging programs are perfect.
cycle.luteal.training: Gradually reduce intensity. Pilates, moderate cardio, and lighter weight training.
cycle.menstruation.nutrition: Increase iron intake (spinach, red meat) and magnesium. Drink ginger tea for cramps.
cycle.follicular.nutrition: Focus on protein and complex carbs. Your body efficiently uses glucose for energy.
cycle.ovulation.nutrition: Anti-inflammatory foods (fish, avocado, blueberries). Hydration is key.
cycle.luteal.nutrition: Increase fiber and magnesium. Reduce salt and sugar. Dark chocolate is okay for sweet cravings!

onboarding.goalFatLossDesc: Burn fat while preserving muscle tone
onboarding.goalFigureDesc: Build curves and sculpt your physique
onboarding.goalBetterHealthDesc: Feel better, move better, live better
onboarding.tempoBalancedSub: Predictable routine, managed obligations, and consistent recovery.
onboarding.tempoActiveSub: Fast-paced, multitasking, with occasional high-pressure periods.
onboarding.tempoHighSub: Frequent deadlines, irregular hours, or physically/mentally taxing routine.
onboarding.expBeginnerDesc: New to training or less than 6 months
onboarding.freq3Desc: Training day, rest day – ideal for your start.
onboarding.freq4Desc: Faster results with a serious approach to your transformation.
onboarding.freq5Desc: Maximum focus for those who prioritize speed of transformation.
onboarding.why.name: We use your name to personalize your experience and communication with your trainer.
onboarding.why.age: Age affects metabolism, recovery speed, and optimal training intensity. This helps us calculate your basal metabolic rate (BMR).
onboarding.why.metrics: Height and weight are essential for calculating your daily calorie needs, BMI, and creating an accurate nutrition plan.
onboarding.why.goal: Your primary goal determines the structure of your training program, calorie targets, and macronutrient ratios.
onboarding.why.metabolic: Metabolic conditions like PCOS or insulin resistance require specific dietary and training adjustments for safe and effective results.
onboarding.why.allergies: We exclude allergens from your meal plan to keep you safe while ensuring proper nutrition.
onboarding.why.limitations: Knowing about injuries helps us avoid exercises that could cause harm and suggest safer alternatives.
onboarding.why.sleep: Sleep quality directly impacts muscle recovery, hormone levels, and training performance. We use this to optimize rest recommendations.
onboarding.why.stress: High stress increases cortisol which affects fat storage and recovery. We adjust training volume based on your stress levels.
onboarding.why.experience: Training experience determines your MEV (Minimum Effective Volume) and MRV (Maximum Recoverable Volume) — critical for programming the right amount of work.
onboarding.why.frequency: How often you can train determines your workout split and ensures each muscle group gets trained optimally throughout the week.
analysis.subtitle: Personalized for {name}
analysis.reasonProgram: Program chosen to match your level of
analysis.reasonMaximize: and maximizes results for your goal
analysis.reasonDeficit: Calories calculated from your BMR and activity level for a gradual deficit
analysis.reasonSurplus: Caloric surplus tailored to your pace for muscle growth
analysis.reasonBalance: Calories balanced for maintenance and general health
analysis.reasonIR: Adapted for insulin resistance — lower carbohydrates
analysis.reasonHighProtein: High protein supports fat loss and protects muscle

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.emptyState: Create your first nutrition template to auto-generate meal plans for clients.
nutrition.cutDesc: Caloric deficit to reduce body fat
nutrition.highProteinDesc: Best for fat loss and muscle retention
nutrition.highCarbDesc: Best for high-intensity training and bulking
nutrition.autoByProfileDesc: Algorithm calculates BMR × activity ± goal adjustment
nutrition.mealStructureInfo: 5 meals ensure optimal protein distribution for muscle protein synthesis activation
nutrition.differentTrainingRest: Different on training vs rest days?
nutrition.restrictionsCaption: These are defaults. Client's personal allergies from onboarding are always applied on top.
nutrition.templateDescPlaceholder: e.g. Moderate deficit for new clients
insight.insulinDesc: Reduced carbohydrates and increased protein ratio. Low glycemic foods prioritized. Gentle deficit to avoid hormonal disruption.
insight.pcosDesc: Anti-inflammatory food focus with omega-3 priority. Moderate carb reduction. Avoiding high-GI foods to manage insulin response.
insight.thyroidDesc: Very gentle caloric adjustment. Aggressive deficits worsen thyroid function by lowering T3 and Heat Shock proteins, triggering metabolic slowdown.
insight.beginnerDesc: Gentle deficit (max 12%) to prevent Jo-Jo effect. The body has not adapted to restriction yet — aggressive cuts trigger survival mode.
insight.stressDesc: Increased protein to counteract cortisol-driven muscle breakdown. Moderate deficit to avoid adding metabolic stress.
insight.synergyVitCIron: Vitamin C enhances iron absorption from this meal
insight.synergyFatVitamins: Healthy fats optimize absorption of fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K)
insight.synergyOmega3: Rich in omega-3: anti-inflammatory, supports brain and heart health
nutrition.proteinWarning: Below recommended minimum for muscle protein synthesis
nutrition.mealStructure5Info: 5 meals ensure optimal protein distribution for muscle protein synthesis (mTOR) activation every 3-4 hours
nutrition.mealStructure3Info: With 3 meals, protein per meal must be higher (40-50g) to reach daily mTOR activation threshold
nutrition.mealStructure4Info: 4 meals provide a good balance of protein distribution with slightly larger portions
nutrition.mealStructure6Info: 6 meals maximize protein synthesis windows but require smaller, more frequent meals
nutrition.resetConfirm: Reset to algorithm-generated plan? Your changes will be lost.

clients.assignProgramHint: Assign a program from Training tab

trainer.overrides.title: Sync Rules Override
trainer.overrides.description: Disable specific sync rules for this client. For 1-on-1 premium clients only.
trainer.overrides.hormonal_sync.title: Luteal carb bonus
trainer.overrides.hormonal_sync.descript: +150 kcal and +38g carbs during luteal phase (not for IR clients).
trainer.overrides.fatigue_sync.title: Fatigue safeguard
trainer.overrides.fatigue_sync.descripti: Switch to maintenance when sleep is low and stress is high.
trainer.overrides.deload_sync.title: Deload nutrition sync
trainer.overrides.deload_sync.descriptio: Switch nutrition to maintenance during training deload week.
trainer.overrides.return_from_break_sync: Soft deficit ramp-up during first sessions after a break.
trainer.overrides.hydration_first.title: Hydration first
trainer.overrides.hydration_first.descri: Block macro changes for 24h when recovery is low and hydration is poor.
Block progression for 3 days when liquid calories exceed 10% of target.
trainer.overrides.illness_penalty.title: Illness penalty
trainer.overrides.illness_penalty.descri: Soft deficit (0.95) during active illness pause.
trainer.overrides.cycle_menstrual_ignore: Skip weight data during menstrual days 1–5 (MA5 unreliable).

checkin.title: Morning check-in
checkin.subtitle: Tell us how you're doing — it helps your plan adapt.
weeklyCheckIn.title: Weekly check-in
weeklyCheckIn.subtitle: A short weekly update helps your plan stay tuned to your body.
weeklyCheckIn.sections.measurementsHint: Optional — leave blank if you prefer.
weeklyCheckIn.fields.weightHint: Prefilled from your daily weigh-ins when available.
weeklyCheckIn.fields.identityHint: How much did this week feel like the version of you you're becoming?
weeklyCheckIn.fields.notesHint: Anything you want to remember about this week — mood, cravings, wins.
weeklyCheckIn.successDesc: Your plan will fine-tune to what your body is doing.
weeklyCheckIn.banner.title: Time for your weekly check-in
weeklyCheckIn.banner.desc: A short weekly update keeps your plan aligned.
banner.luteal.title: Luteal phase — +150 kcal
banner.luteal.desc: Your body needs a bit more energy this week. Target increased.
banner.deload.title: Lighter week — recovery
banner.deload.desc: Less weight, more focus — your nervous system recovers.
banner.returnFromBreak.title: Easing back in
banner.returnFromBreak.desc: The first two sessions are lighter so your body can ease back in.
banner.illness.title: Recovery in progress
banner.illness.desc: Lighter calories and intensity while your immune system bounces back.
banner.hydrationFirst.title: Water first
banner.hydrationFirst.desc: Before we change calories, drink some water — aim for 70%+ of target.
banner.metabolicNoise.title: Let's wait 3 days
banner.metabolicNoise.desc: Lots of liquid calories showing up — plan stays the same until the signal settles.
banner.menstrualWeight.title: Scale can wait
banner.menstrualWeight.desc: Cycle week 1 shows water, not fat — we're skipping adaptation for now.
banner.fatigue.title: Recovery over progress
banner.fatigue.desc: Sleep and stress say: rest. This is maintenance, not a deficit.
home.syncBanner.illness.title: Your body asks for rest
home.syncBanner.illness.subtitle: Easier pace while you recover. Plan is adjusted.
home.syncBanner.deload.title: Deload week
home.syncBanner.deload.subtitle: Recovery week. Muscles grow in calm.
home.syncBanner.luteal.title: Luteal phase: recovery first
home.syncBanner.luteal.subtitle: A bit more carbs, gentler intensity. That's OK.
home.syncBanner.menstrual.title: Menstrual phase
home.syncBanner.menstrual.subtitle: Listen to your body. Today's weight isn't a signal.
preWorkout.dialogSubtitle: Quick check before training — algorithm adapts.
banner.returnFromBreak.subtitle: Tendons and CNS need a week to adapt. Lighter session today.
banner.dietBreak.title: Diet break — 2 weeks
banner.dietBreak.subtitle: Body needs a rest from deficit. Eat normally, train lighter.
banner.overreach.title: Peak week
banner.overreach.subtitle: Heaviest weights. After this comes recovery — push a bit more.
banner.deload.subtitle: Easy session, muscles rebuild. Next week starts strong.
banner.refeed.title: Refuel day
banner.refeed.subtitle: More carbs, less fat — body refuels. Tomorrow normal.
banner.smartCut.title: Plan is adjusting
banner.neat.title: Walk a bit more
mentalReset.replaced.title: It's all good.
mentalReset.replaced.body: Next meal back to plan. One day doesn't change anything — 30 days change everything.
mentalReset.skipped.title: No worries.
mentalReset.skipped.body: Next meal back to plan. No compensation.
weeklyCheckIn.fields.libidoHint: 1 = absent · 10 = strong. A drop is a signal the deficit is too aggressive.
weeklyCheckIn.fields.waterRetentionHint: 1 = none · 10 = puffy. We will not cut carbs — review salt, alcohol, sleep.
